signals.title: AI infrastructure overbuild -- railroad/internet bubble pattern
signals.notes: When infra spend exceeds 2-3% GDP there is almost always a crash. AI capex is at that threshold.

signals.title: China AI dumping threat -- cheap open-weight models kneecap US AI valuations
signals.notes: If large corps adopt cheap Chinese models, site licenses worth billions evaporate. 40% S&P at risk.

signals.title: US market 40-50% correction risk -- AI capex slowdown triggers recession
signals.notes: 40% of S&P directly/tangentially tied to AI bet. Majority of GDP growth last 2 years from AI capex.

signals.title: NVDA: highest AI infrastructure exposure -- most vulnerable to correction
signals.notes: AI chip demand entirely dependent on capex bets that historically crash after infrastructure overbuilds.

signals.title: QQQ: AI-heavy index -- most correlated to AI valuation crash
signals.notes: Nasdaq concentration in AI names makes QQQ ground zero for any AI valuation correction.

signals.title: Crypto risk-off: market crash would hit BTC and alts as investors deleverage
signals.notes: Galloway: 40-50% market correction scenario. Crypto historically amplifies risk-off moves.

signals.title: GLP-1 / healthcare more important than AI long term (Scott Galloway thesis)
signals.notes: Not in current portfolio but noted for future expansion.

signals.title: AI model convergence -- all models heading to same place, pricing power = zero
signals.notes: Models reverse-engineer each others features. Open-weight free models commoditise the space.
